rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER PORTER ET AL VS. COOK GENERAL CONTRACTOR INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint Against Cross-Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 22, 2024 line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT COOK GENERAL CONTRACTOR INC. NOTICE OF MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANTS is GRANTED. Moving party may file its amended cross-complaint naming a new third-party cross-defendant and asserting six causes of action against this cross-defendant. To avoid the virtually inevitable delay in trial prejudicing the Plaintiff, the claims against the new cross-defendant shall be bifurcated and those claims will not be set for October 28, 2024 trial and will be set for Case Management track. However, if the bifurcated claims are ready for trial (answer on file and discovery, including expert discovery, is completed by all parties to the bifurcated cross-complaint), parties may make an ex-parte application no later than October 23, 2024 to consolidate the bifurcated claims with the rest of the case (complaint and cross-complaint). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 22, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER is SUSTAINED, with and without leave to amend, as follows: The First Cause of Action for Exhaustion of Administrative Remedies, is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must state facts demonstrating she exhausted her administrative remedies regarding the City's Anti-blight enforcement decisions. Importantly, the Court notes there has been no final decision for Plaintiff's Building Permit and Variance application. The Second Cause of Action for Exhaustion of Administrative Remedies, is sustained with leave to amend to state a proper cause of action. Plaintiff's claim that the "Exhaustion Doctrine does not and cannot apply," ( 82), does not state a claim for relief. The Third Cause of Action for Falsifying Evidence: DPW submitted false evidence in their Opposition Brief to the Board of Appeals, which is a felony, is sustained without leave to amend for failure to state a valid cause of action. California Penal Code section 134 does not create a private right of action. The Fourth Cause of Action for The City Attorney's Legal Counsel to the Board of Appeals on February 7, 2024, Violates the Equal Protection Clause of the Constitution, is sustained without leave to amend for two reasons: (1) Plaintiff did not present her new claims related to the Board of Appeals hearing on February 7, 2024, in her Government Claim, and (2) the City is immune from liability for actions related to the institution or prosecution of an administrative proceeding under Gov. Code 821.6; permitting decisions under Gov. Code 818.4 and 821.2; and misrepresentations during code enforcement under Gov. Code 818.8 and 822.2. The Fifth Cause of Action for Perjury: DPW Submitted False Testimony to the Board of Appeals on February 7, 2024 is, sustained without leave to amend for failure to state a valid cause of action. California Penal Code sections 118 and 123 do not create a private right of action. The Sixth Cause of Action for Ordinary Mandamus (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085) Financial Conflict of Interest, is sustained with leave to amend to state facts constituting a proper basis for relief; specifically, how Government Code section 87100 applies to the City Attorney's Office, as opposed to an individual "public official" as specified in the statute. The Seventh Cause of Action for Ordinary Mandamus (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085) Ethical Conflict of Interest, is sustained without leave to amend for failure to state a valid basis for relief. Specifically, Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that this court can =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. Tentative ruling continued-see page 2 of 3

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER(FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY PART 2 OF 2
rulings.Rulings: Page 2 of 3 Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 22, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER order the City to "comply with its ethical obligations," namely Rule 1.7 of Professional Conduct. The Eighth Cause of Action for Ordinary Mandamus (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085): Respondent's Anti-Blight Enforcement Policy Denies Citizens their Procedural and Substantive Due Process Rights, is sustained with leave to amend to consolidate the Eighth, Ninth, and Tenth causes of action, which are duplicative of each other. The Ninth Cause of Action for Ordinary Mandamus (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085): Respondent's Anti-Blight Enforcement Policy Denies Citizens Equal Protection of the Law is sustained with leave to amend to consolidate the Eighth, Ninth, and Tenth causes of action, which are duplicative of each other. The Tenth Cause of Action for Writ of Mandate (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085): Government Searches: Anti-Blight Enforcement Searches of Private Property are Unlawful, is sustained with leave to amend to consolidate the Eighth, Ninth, and Tenth causes of action, which are duplicative of each other. The Eleventh Cause of Action for Writ of Mandate (Code of Civ. Proc. 1094.5): SF Admin. Code Chapter 80: Hearings to appeal Violation Notices is sustained with leave to amend to consolidate the Eleventh, Twelfth, Thirteenth, and Fourteenth causes of action, which are duplicative of each other. The Twelfth Cause of Action for Writ of Mandate (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085): SF Admin. Code Chapter 80: DBI Violation Notices is sustained with leave to amend to consolidate the Eleventh, Twelfth, Thirteenth, and Fourteenth causes of action, which are duplicative of each other. The Thirteenth Cause of Action for Writ of Mandate (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085): SF Admin. Code Chapter 80: Planning Violation Notices is sustained with leave to amend to consolidate the Eleventh, Twelfth, Thirteenth, and Fourteenth causes of action, which are duplicative of each other. The Fourteenth Cause of Action for Writ of Mandate (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085): SF Admin. Code Chapter 80: DPW Violation Notices is sustained with leave to amend to consolidate the Eleventh, Twelfth, Thirteenth, and Fourteenth causes of action, which are duplicative of each other. The Fifteenth Cause of Action for DBI, Planning, and DPW Violation Notices Violate Anti-Blight Provisions of the Municipal Code is sustained without leave to amend for failure to state a valid cause of action. A request for civil penalties is a remedy, not a cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. Tentative ruling continued-see page 3 of 3

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO FIFTH AMENDED (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING - PART 3 OF 3)
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO FIFTH AMENDED (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING - PART 3 OF 3) Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 22, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER The Sixteenth Cause of Action for DBI Violation Notices Violate Anti-Blight Provisions of the Municipal Code is sustained without leave to amend for failure to state a valid cause of action. A request for civil penalties is a remedy, not a cause of action. The Seventeenth Cause of Action for Planning Violation Notices Violate Anti-Blight Provisions of the Municipal Code, is sustained without leave to amend for failure to state a valid cause of action. A request for civil penalties is a remedy, not a cause of action. The Eighteenth Cause of Action for DPW and Planning Violation Notices Violate Anti-Blight Provisions of the Municipal Code, is sustained with leave to amend to allege a valid cause of action. A request for civil penalties is a remedy and not a cause of action. To the extent Plaintiff is attempting to state a claim for negligence, Plaintiff has failed to allege sufficient facts constituting a claim for negligence. The Nineteenth Cause of Action for DBI, Planning and DPW Violation Notices Endorse Fraud, a Negligent Mistake of Law and Fact for which the City is Liable, is sustained with leave to amend to state a cause of action. To the extent Plaintiff is attempting to state a claim for fraud, Plaintiff has failed to allege facts sufficient to constitute a claim for fraud. The Twentieth Cause of Action for DBI, Planning and DPW Violation Notices Endorse Extortion, a Negligent Mistake of Law and Fact for which the City is Liable, is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts sufficient to constitute a claim for Extortion. The Twenty-First Cause of Action for DBI, Planning and DPW Violation Notices are an Abuse of Power, a Negligent Mistake of Law and Fact for which the City is Liable, is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege a cause of action for a Monell or Bane Act claim. The Twenty-Second Cause of Action for Ordinary Mandamus (Code of Civ. Proc. 1085) Criminal Profiteering, is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to state a cause of action for criminal profiteering and Racketeer Influences and Corrupt Organizations Act. Defendant's Request for judicial notice of Plaintiff's Fifth Amended Complaint and other exhibits is granted. (Evid. Code, 452(d), (h).) Plaintiff's Request for Written Statement of Decision on Demurrer is denied. (CCP 632.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ABDALLAH ELHANIA ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT KE ZHOU DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING)
rulings.Rulings: Page 2 of 2 Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 22, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT KE ZHOU DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT Demurrer to the cause of action for private nuisance is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts supporting show harm to a property interest. Allegations in paragraph 39 are insufficient ("In addition, after the death of Elias Elhania, Plaintiffs took a prolonged healing trip outside ofCalifornia, trying to heal from the death of their son. This healing trip negatively impacted Plaintiffs economically.") The Court notes that Plaintiff's opposition is devoid of any case authority involving similarly situated plaintiffs bringing a claim for private insurance. (Opposition 4:7-5:1). Demurrer to the cause of action for public nuisance is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of this cause of action. The Court further notes Plaintiff's opposition regarding public nuisance is completely devoid of any case citations pertaining to such cause of action. Plaintiff's position that allegations in paragraph 53 must be taken as true is not supported even by authorities cited by Plaintiff for this proposition as those allegations are conclusions, not facts. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ABDALLAH ELHANIA ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 22, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT The Court notes that Defendant's notice of demurrer states: "Airbnb will, and hereby does, also move that if Plaintiffs' claims survive demurrer, they be stayed pending the arbitration of their claims." This request for relief is not supported by any argument or authority in the Points and Authorities and will not be considered by the Court. The Court further notes that Defendant brings a brand new argument in reply (Reply 8:10-9:6), which will not be considered, plaintiff having no proper opportunity to respond and no explanation provided for only including the argument based on agreement to which the moving defendant was a party since 2018, i.e. for 6 years before the demurrer was filed. Further, request for judicial notice made in a footnote in reply with documents attached to the back of the reply is DENIED. CRC 3.1113(l). Defendant's request for judicial notice of Terms of Service is GRANTED. Demurrer to the cause of action for negligence is OVERRULED. Plaintiff states sufficient facts to support the existence of duty based on Sunnyvale Ordinance designed to ensure that short-term rental are not a nuisance or threaten the public health, safety, or welfare due to disorderly conduct; plusdefendant's involvement in the short-term rental beyond providing a platform for publication. (FAC 18-19). Whether this duty was breached and whether such breach was the cause/proximate cause of Plaintiff's damages is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. Demurrer to the cause of action for private nuisance is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts showing harm to a property interest. Demurrer to the cause of action for public nuisance is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of this cause of action. Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of UCL is OVERRULED. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Sunnyvale ordinance does not apply to the moving defendant as a "person." Defendant does not cite to any definition of "a person" within the Ordinance or elsewhere that would exclude the defendant from the universe of "persons." Further, applicability of the Communications Decency Act is an issue of fact not resolvable on a demurrer. Allegations in the complaint must be taken as true and Plaintiff alleges affirmative acts undertaken by the Defendants (FAC 18-19) going beyond publishing third-party information. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ABDALLAH ELHANIA ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 22, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT KE ZHOU DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT The Court notes that Defendant's demurrer is confusing and fails to comply with the CRC: " The Notice of Demurrer states that Defendant demurs to "the first, second, third, fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action. (Notice 1:23-24). " The Notice of Demurrer does not list any grounds for the demurrer in the opening paragraph. CRC 3.1110(a). " The Demurrer in the opening sentence (starting at "Comes Now") purports to be based on CCP 430.10(e), (f) and (g). (Demurrer 1:26-27) " The demurrer proceeds to only lists causes of action second, third and fourth citing to CCP 430.10(e) as the sole statutory basis. (Demurrer 2:1-12) " Points and Authorities quote subsections (b), (d), (e), and (f) of CCP 430.10 (Points and Authorities 4:3-16). " Points and Authorities address only the second, third and fourth causes of action in the argument section starting at page 5. The Court could overrule the demurrer solely based on the failure to comply with the CRC. However, the Court, finding the common denominator in the inconsistent statements in the Notice, Demurer and Points and Authorities, will address the demurrer based on failure to state sufficient facts, CCP 430.10(e), as to the second (UCL violation), third (private nuisance) and fourth (public nuisance) cause of action. The Court further notes that reply contains new arguments (Reply 2:18-3:11) re: injury suffered as a result as a result of a business dealing with a defendant; new argumentt will not be considered on this demurrer, Plaintiff having not been able to address them. Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of UCL is OVERRULED. Defendant failed to cite any authority for the proposition that Plaintiff's allegations regarding loss of money are insufficient to support standing; or that "reliance" is a required element of the cause of action for UCL violation based on unlawful or unfair prong, citing only to cases involving and arising out of fraud allegations; or why Plaintiff's allegations regarding Defendant's rental activity in violation of the local ordinance is not sufficient to state a "business practice at issue" or "statutes, regulations or other law borrower in support of unlawful claim." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. Tentative ruling continued. see page 2 of 2.
